title: geum oregonense
intro: Geum oregonense, also known as Oregon avens, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Geum oregonense include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as colds and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Geum oregonense is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
